1→# Engineer to order+PLM Component Master - Architecture Context
     2→
     3→## Document Purpose
     4→This document explains the business context, architectural decisions, and design rationale for the **Project Component Master** system in Clevertech's ERP implementation. It serves as a reference for understanding WHY specific design choices were made.
     5→
     6→---
     7→
     8→## Related Documents
     9→
    10→This is part of a complete documentation suite for the Project Component Master system:
    11→
    12→1. **[clevertech_context.md](../clevertech_context.md)** (This Document)
    13→   - Business context and workflow
    14→   - Architectural decisions and rationale
    15→   - Design evolution and trade-offs
    16→   - **Read this first** to understand the "WHY"
    17→
    18→2. **[docs/bom_upload_code_study.md](docs/bom_upload_code_study.md)**
    19→   - Analysis of existing BOM Upload code
    20→   - What can be reused vs. what needs enhancement
    21→   - Integration points for new functionality
    22→   - **Read this second** to understand current implementation
    23→
    24→3. **[docs/project_component_master_tech_specs.md](docs/project_component_master_tech_specs.md)**
    25→   - Complete DocType JSON definitions
    26→   - All function specifications with signatures
    27→   - Test cases and validation logic
    28→   - Implementation checklist
    29→   - **Use this for implementation** - contains all technical details
    30→
    31→**Recommended Reading Order:**
    32→- **Manager/Stakeholder:** Read Context doc only
    33→- **Developer:** Read all three in order (Context → Code Study → Tech Specs)
    34→- **QA/Tester:** Focus on Context doc + Test Cases section in Tech Specs
    35→
    36→---
    37→
    38→## Table of Contents
    39→1. [Business Context](#business-context)
    40→2. [Current System & Limitations](#current-system--limitations)
    41→3. [Core Requirements](#core-requirements)
    42→4. [Architectural Decisions](#architectural-decisions)
    43→5. [Design Evolution](#design-evolution)
    44→6. [Final Architecture](#final-architecture)
    45→
    46→---
    47→
    48→## Business Context
    49→
    50→### Company Profile
    51→**Clevertech** is an engineer-to-order (ETO) manufacturer of packaging machines. Their business model is characterized by:
    52→- Custom machine design per customer order
    53→- Long lead times for component procurement
    54→- Complex multi-level Bill of Materials (BOM)
    55→- Continuous design evolution during project execution
    56→
    57→### Current Workflow
    58→
    59→#### 1. Design Phase
    60→- Machines are designed in **SolidWorks** (3D CAD)
    61→- Bill of Materials (BOM) is maintained in **PE2** (PLM system)
    62→- A typical machine has **~500 components** across multiple levels
    63→- Not all components are designed upfront
    64→
    65→#### 2. Phased Design Release
    66→**Day 1:**
    67→- Only 2-3 components finalized
    68→- These components may have 4-level deep BOMs
    69→- Procurement must start immediately due to long lead times
    70→
    71→**Day 30:**
    72→- 3 more components released
    73→- Some existing components may have design revisions
    74→- Procurement for initial components already in progress
    75→
    76→**Day 60+:**
    77→- Continuous component additions and revisions
    78→- Some components procured as "loose items" before design finalized
    79→
    80→#### 3. Current ERPNext Integration
    81→**Process:**
    82→1. Export BOM from PE2 to Excel
    83→2. Upload Excel to ERPNext via "BOM Upload" doctype
    84→3. System creates Item masters and BOM structures
    85→4. Material Requests (MR) raised from BOMs
    86→5. Standard procurement workflow: MR → RFQ → Supplier Quotation → Purchase Order
    87→
    88→**Problem:** This works for initial upload but breaks down on subsequent uploads when components change.
    89→
    90→---
    91→
    92→## Current System & Limitations
    93→
    94→### Existing BOM Upload Logic
    95→
    96→**What it does:**
    97→```python
    98→1. Parse Excel file (specific column mapping from PE2 export)
    99→2. Build hierarchical tree structure from "level" column
   100→3. Create Item masters if they don't exist
   101→4. Create BOMs recursively (bottom-up approach)
   102→5. Skip if BOM exists (simple existence check)
   103→```
   104→
   105→**Critical Limitations:**
   106→
   107→#### Limitation 1: Blind Skipping
   108→```python
   109→# Current logic
   110→existing = frappe.db.exists("BOM", {
   111→    "item": item_code,
   112→    "is_active": 1,
   113→    "is_default": 1
   114→})
   115→
   116→if existing:
   117→    return False  # Skip, no questions asked
   118→```
   119→
   120→**Problem:** If Motor-Assembly BOM changed from 3 child items to 5 child items, system silently skips it. Procurement happens with outdated BOM.
   121→
   122→#### Limitation 2: No Change Detection
   123→- No comparison of BOM structure
   124→- No alerting when components change
   125→- No tracking of what changed (added/removed/qty modified)
   126→
   127→#### Limitation 3: No Procurement Tracking
   128→- Can't see which components have procurement initiated
   129→- No prevention of duplicate procurement
   130→- No visibility into "loose items" that later get added to BOMs
   131→
   132→#### Limitation 4: No Version Management
   133→- When BOM changes, old BOM just replaced
   134→- No audit trail of versions
   135→- Procurement documents may reference obsolete BOMs
   136→
   137→#### Limitation 5: No Budget/Timeline Tracking
   138→- No way to set target costs per component
   139→- No target delivery dates
   140→- No rolled-up cost estimates for assemblies
   141→
   142→#### Limitation 6: Loose Item Gap
   143→**Scenario:**
   144→```
   145→Day 1: Bearing (long lead time) → Procure 100 units as "loose item"
   146→Day 30: Motor-Assembly BOM uploaded → Requires Bearing × 50 units
   147→       → System creates new Material Request for 50 units
   148→       → Result: 150 units procured (50 excess!)
   149→```
   150→
   151→No mechanism to track that Bearing was already procured outside BOM context.
   152→
   153→---
   154→
   155→## Core Requirements
   156→
   157→### Requirement 1: Incremental BOM Updates
   158→**Need:** Upload full BOM export monthly/weekly, but only create NEW components, skip unchanged, and ALERT on changes.
   159→
   160→**Business Driver:** Design evolves continuously. Can't manually track 500 components across 20 projects.
   161→
   162→### Requirement 2: Change Detection with Impact Analysis
   163→**Need:** When Motor-Gearbox BOM changes, system must:
   164→- Detect the change (compare structure)
   165→- Find all parent assemblies using Motor-Gearbox
   166→- Block creation of entire chain until resolved
   167→- Show user what changed (qty, new items, removed items)
   168→
   169→**Business Driver:** Cost rollups and procurement tracking break if parent BOMs created with wrong child structure.
   170→
   171→### Requirement 3: Loose Item Management
   172→**Need:** Track components procured before design finalized:
   173→- Allow procurement without BOM
   174→- When component added to BOM later, prevent duplicate procurement
   175→- Track quantity limits: `MAX(loose_qty, bom_qty)` not `SUM`
   176→
   177→**Business Driver:** Long lead items (6+ months) must be ordered before design complete. Common in custom machinery.
   178→
   179→### Requirement 4: Version Control
   180→**Need:** When Motor-Assembly BOM changes:
   181→- Create new version (not replace)
   182→- Keep old version for existing POs
   183→- Track procurement per version
   184→- Allow parallel procurement (v1 and v2 simultaneously)
   185→
   186→**Business Driver:** May have orders for old version in production while new version being quoted.
   187→
   188→### Requirement 5: Budget & Timeline Tracking
   189→**Need:**
   190→- Set target cost per component (designer estimate)
   191→- Auto-calculate rolled-up cost from child components
   192→- Allow manual override (for assembly labor, overhead)
   193→- Track actual procurement cost vs budget
   194→
   195→**Business Driver:** Custom machines quoted based on component cost estimates. Need variance tracking.
   196→
   197→### Requirement 6: Procurement Quantity Control
   198→**Need:** Hard limit on Material Request quantities:
   199→```
   200→If Bearing: loose_qty=100, bom_qty=150
   201→Then: total_limit = MAX(100, 150) = 150
   202→Block any MR that would cause total procurement > 150
   203→```
   204→
   205→**Business Driver:** Prevent over-procurement. Inventory carrying costs are high for custom components.
   206→
   207→### Requirement 7: Comprehensive Reporting
   208→**Need:** Report showing per project:
   209→- All components (assemblies + loose items)
   210→- BOM hierarchy (levels)
   211→- Budget vs actual
   212→- Procurement status (Not Started / In Progress / Complete)
   213→- Linked documents (MR, RFQ, PO)
   214→
   215→**Business Driver:** Management needs project-level procurement visibility for cash flow planning.
   216→
   217→---
   218→
   219→## Architectural Decisions
   220→
   221→### Decision 1: Introduce "Project Component Master" DocType
   222→
   223→**Problem:** ERPNext's native Item and BOM doctypes are project-agnostic. Can't track project-specific:
   224→- Procurement status per project
   225→- Budget per project (same bearing may have different target cost in different projects)
   226→- Loose item tracking per project
   227→
   228→**Solution:** New master doctype bridging Project ↔ Item ↔ BOM
   229→
   230→**Why not use existing doctypes?**
   231→
   232→| Option | Why Rejected |
   233→|--------|--------------|
   234→| **Custom fields on Item** | Items are global; same item used across projects with different budgets/status |
   235→| **Custom fields on BOM** | BOMs are global; same BOM may be used in multiple projects |
   236→| **Project doctype child table** | Can't handle deep hierarchy; poor query performance; limited customization |
   237→| **New DocType** | ✓ Project-specific context, ✓ Rich data model, ✓ Separate permissions |
   238→
   239→**Trade-off Accepted:** Additional complexity of maintaining Component Master, but gains flexibility and clarity.
   240→
   241→---
   242→
   243→### Decision 2: Component Master Scope - "ALL Items in BOM Tree"
   244→
   245→> **⚠️ EVOLVED:** Original design (v1.0-v1.7) was "Assemblies + Loose Items only". Changed in v1.9 to include ALL items after realizing raw materials need procurement validation and reporting rollup.
   246→
   247→**Evolution of this Decision:**
   248→
   249→**v1.0–v1.7: Assemblies + Loose Items Only**
   250→- Only items with BOMs or marked as loose got Component Masters
   251→- Raw materials (leaf items) were NOT tracked
   252→- **Problem discovered:** MR/PO validation couldn't enforce limits on raw materials because they had no Component Master record!
   253→- **Problem discovered:** Management report needed rollup from any level (M → G → D → RM), requiring all levels to exist
   254→
   255→**v1.9: ALL Items in BOM Tree (CURRENT DESIGN)**
   256→```
   257→Project Component Master contains:
   258→✓ Root assemblies (M level) — has_bom=1, make_or_buy=Make
   259→✓ Sub-assemblies (G level) — has_bom=1, make_or_buy=Make
   260→✓ Sub-assemblies (D level) — has_bom=1, make_or_buy=Make OR Buy
   261→✓ Raw materials (RM level) — has_bom=0, make_or_buy=Buy
   262→✓ Loose raw materials — is_loose_item=1
   263→```
   264→
   265→**Why ALL items now:**
   266→1. **Procurement validation:** MR/PO for raw materials needs Component Master to check limits
   267→2. **Report rollup:** Management views at M/G level require all children to exist for aggregation
   268→3. **Calculation chain:** `project_qty` propagates from root → children; every level needs a record
   269→4. **Make/Buy control:** Parent's make/buy flag determines if children need separate procurement
   270→5. **Consistent data model:** Every item in the project has one tracking record
   271→
   272→**Trade-off Accepted:**
   273→- More records (~200-500 per project instead of ~50-100)
   274→- ERPNext handles this scale well
   275→- Benefits of complete visibility outweigh storage cost
   276→
   277→---
   278→
   279→### Decision 3: Hierarchical Structure (Parent-Child)
   280→
   281→**Options Considered:**
   282→
   283→**Option A: Flat List**
   284→```
   285→Component Master:
   286→- Motor-Assembly (no parent link)
   287→- Gearbox-Sub (no parent link)
   288→- Shaft-001 (no parent link)
   289→```
   290→
   291→**Option B: Hierarchical**
   292→```
   293→Component Master:
   294→- Motor-Assembly (parent=null, level=0)
   295→- Gearbox-Sub (parent=Motor-Assembly, level=1)
   296→- Custom-Bracket (parent=Gearbox-Sub, level=2)
   297→```
   298→
   299→**Chosen: Option B (Hierarchical)**
   300→
   301→**Rationale:**
   302→1. **Cost Rollup:** Need to calculate Motor-Assembly cost = Gearbox cost + Housing cost + ...
   303→2. **Dependency Tracking:** If Gearbox changes, must know Motor-Assembly affected
   304→3. **Reporting:** Show "which assemblies are blocked by Gearbox delay?"
   305→4. **Business Context:** PE2 export has hierarchy (level column), natural to preserve it
   306→
   307→**Implementation Detail:**
   308→- Hierarchy implicit in PE2 export (level 0, 1, 2...)
   309→- BOM Upload derives parent-child during tree building
   310→- Auto-populates `parent_component` and `bom_level` fields
   311→
   312→---
   313→
   314→### Decision 3A: BOM Versioning and Change Propagation
   315→
   316→**CRITICAL UNDERSTANDING: BOMs are Version-Locked**
   317→
   318→**How ERPNext BOMs Work:**
   319→```
   320→Parent BOMs reference children by ITEM CODE with VERSION FROZEN at submission:
   321→
   322→BOM-A-001 (submitted):
   323→  Items:
   324→    - Item: B (qty: 1)  → Uses BOM-B-001 (frozen)
   325→
   326→BOM-C-001 (submitted):
   327→  Items:
   328→    - Item: B (qty: 3)  → Uses BOM-B-001 (frozen)
   329→```
   330→
   331→**When Child BOM Changes:**
   332→
   333→**Scenario:** Design team updates item B's structure in SolidWorks → PE2 → New Excel export
   334→
   335→**What Happens:**
   336→1. New Excel file contains:
   337→   - Updated BOM for item B
   338→   - BOM for item A (which uses B)
   339→   - BOM for item C (which uses B)
   340→
   341→2. BOM Upload creates NEW versions:
   342→   - BOM-B-002 (new structure for B)
   343→   - BOM-A-002 (uses BOM-B-002)
   344→   - BOM-C-002 (uses BOM-B-002)
   345→
   346→3. Old versions become inactive:
   347→   - BOM-A-001 still exists but inactive
   348→   - BOM-B-001 still exists but inactive
   349→   - BOM-C-001 still exists but inactive
   350→
   351→**Key Insight:**
   352→- Parent BOMs **DO NOT** automatically use new child BOM versions
   353→- BOMs are **IMMUTABLE** after submission
   354→- Change propagates via **complete BOM Upload** (all affected BOMs created with new versions)
   355→- This is why PE2 exports the ENTIRE hierarchy (not just changed items)
   356→
   357→**Impact on Component Master:**
   358→
   359→```
   360→Before BOM Upload (Old State):
   361→Component Master for B:
   362→├── active_bom = BOM-B-001
   363→├── BOM Usage Table:
   364→    ├── parent_bom = BOM-A-001
   365→    └── parent_bom = BOM-C-001
   366→
   367→After BOM Upload (New State):
   368→Component Master for B:
   369→├── active_bom = BOM-B-002  ← UPDATED
   370→├── BOM Usage Table:
   371→    ├── parent_bom = BOM-A-002  ← UPDATED (old row removed, new row added)
   372→    └── parent_bom = BOM-C-002  ← UPDATED (old row removed, new row added)
   373→```
   374→
   375→**Blocking Logic:**
   376→```
   377→When BOM Upload attempts to create BOM-B-002:
   378→
   379→1. Check: Does Component Master for B exist? → YES
   380→2. Check: Does B have active procurement? → Check procurement_records table
   381→3. If YES (procurement exists):
   382→   - BLOCK entire BOM Upload
   383→   - Prevents creation of BOM-A-002, BOM-B-002, BOM-C-002
   384→   - Show user: "Item B has procurement. Cannot change. Affects BOM-A-001, BOM-C-001"
   385→   - User must resolve/cancel procurement before uploading new file
   386→
   387→4. If NO (no procurement):
   388→   - Allow BOM Upload
   389→   - Create BOM-A-002, BOM-B-002, BOM-C-002
   390→   - Deactivate BOM-A-001, BOM-B-001, BOM-C-001
   391→   - Update Component Master BOM Usage tables
   392→```
   393→
   394→**Why This Matters:**
   395→1. **Procurement Control:** A single procured item blocks entire hierarchy update
   396→2. **Change Traceability:** BOM versions track exact configuration at point in time
   397→3. **Surgical Blocking:** Only affected items with procurement block upload
   398→4. **Unaffected Siblings:** If item D (not in hierarchy) has procurement, it doesn't block A/B/C changes
   399→
   400→**Rationale:**
   401→- ERPNext's BOM immutability prevents mid-procurement confusion
   402→- PE2's full hierarchy export ensures all parent references update together
   403→- Component Master tracks current active versions + parent-child relationships
   404→- Blocking logic prevents breaking existing procurement commitments
   405→
   406→---
   407→
   408→### Decision 4: Loose Item Conversion Logic
   409→
   410→**Key Insight:** Loose items are **always raw materials**, never become assemblies with BOMs.
   411→
   412→**Status States (Based on Actual BOM Usage):**
   413→```
   414→State 1: Pending Conversion
   415→- is_loose_item = Yes
   416→- has_bom = No
   417→- bom_conversion_status = Pending Conversion
   418→- bom_usage table is empty (not used in any BOM yet)
   419→
   420→State 2: Converted to BOM
   421→- is_loose_item = Yes (preserves history)
   422→- has_bom = No (still a raw material)
   423→- bom_conversion_status = Converted to BOM
   424→- bom_usage table has 1 entry (used in exactly one BOM)
   425→
   426→State 3: Partial (Used in Multiple BOMs)
   427→- is_loose_item = Yes
   428→- has_bom = No
   429→- bom_conversion_status = Partial
   430→- bom_usage table has 2+ entries (used in multiple BOMs)
   431→```
   432→
   433→**Important:** Status is determined by **actual BOM usage**, not by the checkbox.
   434→
   435→**Permission Gate (Separate from Status):**
   436→```
   437→can_be_converted_to_bom (checkbox):
   438→- Default: No (unchecked)
   439→- Purpose: Permission gate to control BOM Upload
   440→- Does NOT change status
   441→- If No: BOM Upload BLOCKED (hard error)
   442→- If Yes: BOM Upload ALLOWED (status updates when actually used)
   443→```
   444→
   445→**Blocking Logic:**
   446→```python
   447→if is_loose_item and not can_be_converted_to_bom:
   448→    # HARD BLOCK - cannot use in any BOM
   449→    raise Exception("Enable conversion first")
   450→```
   451→
   452→**Why keep `is_loose_item=Yes` even after conversion?**
   453→- Preserves procurement history
   454→- Loose vs BOM procurement tracked separately
   455→- Helps explain why total_qty_limit might be higher than BOM requirement
   456→
   457→**Rationale:** Designer must explicitly confirm "design finalized, OK to use in production BOM" by checking the box, but status only changes when item is actually added to a BOM.
   458→
   459→---
   460→
   461→### Decision 5: Procurement Quantity Control - MAX not SUM
   462→
   463→**Critical Business Logic:**
   464→
   465→**Scenario:**
   466→```
   467→Bearing-SKF-001:
   468→- Day 1: Loose procurement = 100 units (long lead time)
   469→- Day 30: Motor-Assembly BOM = 50 units
   470→- Day 45: Pump-Assembly BOM = 30 units
   471→- Total BOM requirement: 80 units
   472→```
   473→
   474→**Question:** What's the procurement limit?
   475→
   476→**Wrong Answer: SUM**
   477→```
   478→Total limit = 100 (loose) + 80 (BOM) = 180
   479→Problem: Bearing procured twice! 100 loose + 80 from BOM = 180 units
   480→But only need MAX(100, 80) = 100 units
   481→```
   482→
   483→**Correct Answer: MAX**
   484→```
   485→Total limit = MAX(100, 80) = 100 units
   486→Rationale:
   487→- If loose=100 and BOM=80: Loose already covers BOM, no additional procurement
   488→- If loose=100 and BOM=150: Need 150 total, loose covers 100, procure 50 more
   489→```
   490→
   491→**Implementation:**
   492→```python
   493→total_qty_limit = MAX(loose_qty_required, bom_qty_required)
   494→
   495→# Material Request Validation
   496→if total_procurement_attempt > total_qty_limit:
   497→    raise Exception("Exceeds limit")
   498→```
   499→
   500→**Edge Case Handling:**
   501→```
   502→If loose=200, BOM=100:
   503→- total_limit = MAX(200, 100) = 200 ✓
   504→- But BOM MR should be 0 (already over-procured!)
   505→- System shows: "Warning: Loose procurement (200) exceeds BOM (100)"
   506→- Allows MR up to 200 total, not 200 + 100
   507→```
   508→
   509→---
   510→
   511→### Decision 6: Change Detection - Hash-Based Comparison
   512→
   513→**Problem:** How to detect if BOM structure changed?
   514→
   515→**Options Considered:**
   516→
   517→**Option A: Field-by-Field Comparison**
   518→```python
   519→for each child:
   520→    compare item_code, qty, uom, custom_fields...
   521→```
   522→Rejected: Slow, complex, miss changes if new field added
   523→
   524→**Option B: BOM Version Number**
   525→```python
   526→if old_bom.version != new_bom.version:
   527→    changed = True
   528→```
   529→Rejected: PE2 doesn't export version cleanly, prone to false positives
   530→
   531→**Option C: Structure Hash (CHOSEN)**
   532→```python
   533→structure = sorted([(item_code, qty) for child in children])
   534→hash = md5(str(structure))
   535→```
   536→
   537→**Rationale:**
   538→- Fast comparison (single string match)
   539→- Captures structural changes (items added/removed/qty changed)
   540→- Ignores non-structural changes (descriptions, custom fields)
   541→- Same approach used by existing duplicate detection validation
   542→
   543→**What triggers "changed"?**
   544→- ✓ Child item added
   545→- ✓ Child item removed
   546→- ✓ Child item quantity changed
   547→- ✗ Child item description changed (not structural)
   548→- ✗ Custom fields changed (position, revision, material)
   549→
   550→**Trade-off:** May miss "soft" changes (revision updated but BOM structure same). Acceptable because revision tracked separately in BOM Item custom fields.
   551→
   552→---
   553→
   554→### Decision 7: Change Blocking Strategy - Ancestor Chain
   555→
   556→**Problem:** When Gearbox BOM changes, what should be blocked?
   557→
   558→**Options Considered:**
   559→
   560→**Option A: Block Entire Upload (All or Nothing)**
   561→```
   562→If ANY component changed → Block ALL components
   563→```
   564→Rejected: Unnecessarily delays 98 components because 2 changed
   565→
   566→**Option B: Block Only Changed Component**
   567→```
   568→Block: Gearbox
   569→Allow: Motor-Assembly (uses Gearbox)
   570→```
   571→Rejected: Creates Motor-Assembly BOM with wrong Gearbox structure
   572→
   573→**Option C: Block Ancestor Chain (CHOSEN)**
   574→```
   575→If Gearbox changed:
   576→- Block Gearbox ✓
   577→- Block Motor-Assembly (uses Gearbox) ✓
   578→- Block Main-Machine (uses Motor-Assembly) ✓
   579→- Allow Pump-Assembly (doesn't use Gearbox) ✓
   580→```
   581→
   582→**Algorithm:**
   583→```python
   584→1. Identify changed components
   585→2. For each changed component:
   586→   - Find all ancestors (components containing it, recursively)
   587→   - Add ancestors to blocked set
   588→3. Allow creation of components NOT in blocked set
   589→```
   590→
   591→**Why this approach?**
   592→- **Surgical:** Only blocks what's affected
   593→- **Safe:** Preserves BOM integrity (no partial structures)
   594→- **Efficient:** Allows parallel work on unrelated components
   595→
   596→**Multi-Level Example:**
   597→```
   598→Upload contains:
   599→- Machine
   600→  ├── Module-A
   601→  │   └── Assembly-X (unchanged)
   602→  └── Module-B
   603→      └── Gearbox (CHANGED)
   604→
   605→Result:
   606→✓ Allow: Assembly-X (no dependency on Gearbox)
   607→✗ Block: Gearbox (changed itself)
   608→✗ Block: Module-B (contains Gearbox)
   609→✗ Block: Machine (contains Module-B which contains Gearbox)
   610→```
   611→
   612→---
   613→
   614→### Decision 8: Budget Tracking - No Child Table
   615→
   616→**Problem:** Need to track budget at component level AND leaf item level for cost rollup.
   617→
   618→**Options Considered:**
   619→
   620→**Option A: Budget Child Table in Component Master**
   621→```
   622→Project Component Master: Motor-Assembly
   623→├── budgeted_rate: $500 (parent level)
   624→└── Child Table: Item Budgets
   625→    ├── Shaft: $100
   626→    ├── Bearing: $50
   627→    └── Housing: $200
   628→```
   629→
   630→**Rejected because:**
   631→- Duplication: BOM already has item list
   632→- Sync issues: If BOM changes, budget table out of sync
   633→- Maintenance burden: Manual entry for every child item
   634→- Complexity: What if child item also has BOM? Recursive budgets?
   635→
   636→**Option B: Budget in BOM Item (ERPNext child table)**
   637→```
   638→BOM: Motor-Assembly
   639→└── BOM Items:
   640→    ├── Shaft, qty=1, custom_budgeted_rate=$100
   641→    ├── Bearing, qty=2, custom_budgeted_rate=$50
   642→```
   643→
   644→**Rejected because:**
   645→- Budget lives in BOM, not Component Master
   646→- If multiple BOM versions, which budget applies?
   647→- Hard to report "all component budgets" without querying BOMs
   648→
   649→**Option C: Budget Only for Managed Components (CHOSEN)**
   650→```
   651→Project Component Master:
   652→├── Motor-Assembly: budgeted_rate=$500 (manual entry)
   653→├── Gearbox-Sub: budgeted_rate=$300 (manual entry)
   654→└── Custom-Bracket: budgeted_rate=$150 (loose item)
   655→
   656→Rollup Logic:
   657→Motor-Assembly cost =
   658→  Gearbox-Sub budget ($300 from Component Master) +
   659→  Bearing actual ($120 from last PO) +
   660→  Bolts actual ($8 from last PO)
   661→```
   662→
   663→**Rationale:**
   664→- Budgets only for "managed components" (assemblies + loose items)
   665→- Leaf catalog items (bolts, bearings) use actual procurement cost
   666→- Designer estimates at assembly level, not bolt level
   667→- Rollup combines budgets (for managed) + actuals (for catalog items)
   668→
   669→**Business Context:**
   670→- ETO quoting done at assembly level ("Motor section: $5000")
   671→- Not at leaf level ("M6 bolt: $0.05")
   672→- This matches how designers think about costs
   673→
   674→---
   675→
   676→### Decision 9: Material Request Validation - Hard Block
   677→
   678→**Problem:** How to enforce procurement quantity limits?
   679→
   680→**Options Considered:**
   681→
   682→**Option A: Warning Only**
   683→```python
   684→if mr_qty > limit:
   685→    frappe.msgprint("Warning: Exceeds limit")
   686→    # But allow save
   687→```
   688→Rejected: Users ignore warnings, over-procurement happens
   689→
   690→**Option B: Auto-Adjust**
   691→```python
   692→if mr_qty > limit:
   693→    mr_qty = max_allowed
   694→    frappe.msgprint("Adjusted to maximum")
   695→```
   696→Rejected: Silent changes confusing, may hide data entry errors
   697→
   698→**Option C: Hard Block (CHOSEN)**
   699→```python
   700→if mr_qty > limit:
   701→    frappe.throw("Cannot exceed limit")
   702→    # Save fails
   703→```
   704→
   705→**Rationale:**
   706→- Over-procurement is costly (inventory carrying, obsolescence)
   707→- Better to force user decision than allow mistake
   708→- User can increase limit in Component Master if genuinely needed
   709→- Clear error message explains why and what limit is
   710→
   711→**UX Consideration:**
   712→Error message shows:
   713→```
   714→Cannot add 200 units of Bearing-SKF to Material Request
   715→
   716→Procurement Limit Exceeded:
   717→• Total Limit: 150 (from Component Master)
   718→• Existing MRs: 100
   719→• This MR: 200
   720→• Total: 300
   721→
   722→Maximum allowed for this MR: 50
   723→```
   724→
   725→Gives user actionable information to fix.
   726→
   727→---
   728→
   729→### Decision 10: BOM Versioning - Use ERPNext Native
   730→
   731→**Decision:** Don't build custom versioning, use ERPNext's native BOM versioning.
   732→
   733→**Rationale:**
   734→- ERPNext already supports multiple BOMs per item
   735→- Activate/deactivate mechanism exists
   736→- Reports and queries understand BOM status
   737→- Less code to maintain
   738→
   739→**Component Master Role:**
   740→- Tracks `active_bom` (current version)
   741→- Child table tracks BOM version history
   742→- Provides project-specific version context
   743→
   744→**When BOM changes:**
   745→```
   746→Old approach (custom): BOM-MOT-001-V1, BOM-MOT-001-V2
   747→New approach (native): BOM-MOT-001, BOM-MOT-002 (ERPNext auto-naming)
   748→
   749→Component Master:
   750→- active_bom = BOM-MOT-002
   751→- BOM Version History:
   752→  - BOM-MOT-001 (v1, created 2026-01-01, procurement: PO-001)
   753→  - BOM-MOT-002 (v2, created 2026-02-15, procurement: PO-045)
   754→```
   755→
   756→**User Workflow:**
   757→1. Upload detects change → Show dialog
   758→2. User manually creates new BOM (or lets system create with new name)
   759→3. User deactivates old BOM
   760→4. System updates Component Master.active_bom
   761→
   762→---
   763→
   764→### Decision 11: Two Distinct Flows for Component Master Creation
   765→
   766→**CRITICAL: Component Masters are created differently depending on the scenario.**
   767→
   768→**Flow 1: Cutover (Existing Projects)**
   769→```
   770→Projects where BOMs already exist in ERPNext (e.g., SMR260001 with 24 BOMs).
   771→
   772→Sequence:
   773→1. BOMs already exist (submitted, active, default)
   774→2. User clicks "Generate Component Masters" on Project form
   775→3. System creates Component Masters FROM existing BOMs
   776→4. Sets: has_bom=1, active_bom=BOM-xxx, bom_structure_hash, design_status="Design Released"
   777→5. Populates BOM Usage tables retroactively
   778→
   779→Direction: BOM → Component Master (retrospective)
   780→Implementation: Phase 2A — bulk_generation.py + project.js
   781→```
   782→
   783→**Flow 2: New Projects (Ongoing PE2 Uploads)**
   784→```
   785→New project, first PE2 export uploaded to BOM Upload doctype.
   786→
   787→Sequence:
   788→1. Parse PE2 Excel → build tree of assemblies
   789→2. Create Component Masters FIRST (has_bom=1, active_bom=null)
   790→3. Run analysis — check procurement blocks, loose items, changes
   791→4. Create BOMs bottom-up (existing BOM Upload logic)
   792→5. Link back — set active_bom and bom_structure_hash on Component Masters
   793→6. BOM on_submit hooks fire → populate BOM Usage tables automatically
   794→
   795→Direction: Component Master → BOM (proactive)
   796→Implementation: Phase 3 — enhanced BOM Upload
   797→```
   798→
   799→**Why Component Masters BEFORE BOMs in Flow 2:**
   800→- **Blocking logic needs them:** During BOM creation, the system checks if a component has active procurement or is a loose item without conversion enabled. These checks require the Component Master to already exist.
   801→- **Analysis requires them:** The analyze_upload() function queries Component Masters to categorize items as new/unchanged/changed/blocked.
   802→- **active_bom is set later:** Component Master is created with active_bom=null, then updated after BOM is successfully created and submitted.
   803→
   804→**Why BOMs BEFORE Component Masters in Flow 1:**
   805→- **No analysis needed:** Cutover data is already clean — BOMs are submitted and active.
   806→- **No blocking possible:** No procurement exists yet for these items.
   807→- **Simpler:** Just read existing BOMs and create tracking records.
   808→
   809→**Incremental Upload (Day 30, Day 60...):**
   810→```
   811→Subsequent PE2 uploads for an existing project follow Flow 2:
   812→
   813→1. Parse Excel → build tree
   814→2. Some items already have Component Masters (from previous upload)
   815→3. New items: Create Component Masters first, then BOMs
   816→4. Unchanged items: Skip (hash comparison)
   817→5. Changed items: Block ancestor chain, require resolution
   818→6. BOM hooks update existing Component Masters automatically
   819→```
   820→
   821→**Rationale:**
   822→- Cutover is a one-time operation, simplicity wins
   823→- Ongoing uploads need full validation pipeline, safety wins
   824→- Both flows produce the same end state: Component Master with active_bom linked
   825→
   826→---
   827→
   828→### Decision 12: New Button on BOM Upload — Reuse Existing Code via Imports
   829→
   830→**CRITICAL: Do NOT modify the existing BOM Upload code written by another developer.**
   831→
   832→**Strategy: Option B — New button, import their functions**
   833→
   834→**Existing BOM Upload code** (`clevertech/doctype/bom_upload/bom_upload.py`) provides:
   835→```
   836→Importable Functions:
   837→├── parse_rows(ws)                              → Excel worksheet → list of row dicts
   838→├── build_tree(rows)                            → Row list → nested tree (stack-based, uses "level" field)
   839→├── ensure_item_exists(item_code, desc, uom)    → Create Item master if missing
   840→├── create_bom_recursive(node, project, root)   → Bottom-up BOM creation + child linking
   841→└── create_boms(docname)                        → Main entry point (existing button calls this)
   842→
   843→Helper Functions:
   844→├── clean_code(code)                            → Remove dots, strip whitespace from item codes
   845→├── to_float(val, default)                      → Safe float conversion
   846→└── normalize_uom(uom)                          → NUMERI→Nos, PEZZI→Nos, METRI→Meter
   847→```
   848→
   849→**Key finding:** Item creation happens INSIDE `create_bom_recursive()` — it calls
   850→`ensure_item_exists()` for each node before creating the BOM. Item + BOM creation
   851→are interleaved, not separate phases.
   852→
   853→**Our new button's sequence (using their imports):**
   854→```
   855→Step 1: parse_rows(ws)                          → Their code — parse Excel
   856→Step 2: build_tree(rows)                        → Their code — build hierarchy
   857→Step 3: Walk tree → ensure_item_exists()        → Their code — Items must exist before
   858→         for all assembly nodes                   Component Masters (Link field dependency)
   859→Step 4: Create Component Masters                → OUR code — assemblies only, active_bom=null
   860→Step 5: Run analysis                            → OUR code — hash comparison, blocking checks
   861→Step 6: create_bom_recursive()                  → Their code — bottom-up BOM creation
   862→         (calls ensure_item_exists again,          (harmless — checks existence, returns early)
   863→          for leaf items too)
   864→Step 7: Link active_bom back to                 → OUR code — set active_bom + hash
   865→         Component Masters
   866→```
   867→
   868→**Dependency chain:**
   869→```
   870→Items must exist       → before Component Masters  (item_code is Link field to Item)
   871→Component Masters must → before analysis           (blocking checks query Component Master)
   872→exist
   873→Analysis must pass     → before BOM creation        (blocked = stop, don't create BOMs)
   874→BOMs must exist        → before linking active_bom   (need BOM name to set on Component Master)
   875→```
   876→
   877→**Why this works without touching their code:**
   878→- Their existing "Create BOMs" button continues to work as-is
   879→- Our new "Create BOMs with Validation" button imports their functions
   880→- `ensure_item_exists()` is idempotent (checks first, skips if exists)
   881→- `create_bom_recursive()` is idempotent (skips if BOM already active/default)
   882→- Users choose which button to use: old (no tracking) or new (with Component Masters)
   883→
   884→---
   885→
   886→### Decision 13: Project Quantity Multiplication and Simplified Procurement Limit (FINAL DESIGN)
   887→
   888→**Problem Identified:** `bom_qty_required` was summing raw `qty_per_unit` from BOM Usage table without accounting for how many units of the parent assembly the project actually needs.
   889→
   890→**Example of the Bug:**
   891→```
   892→BOM-A (project needs 2 units)
   893→├── RM-1 (qty_per_unit = 2 per unit of A)
   894→└── RM-2 (qty_per_unit = 5 per unit of A)
   895→
   896→❌ WRONG (old):  bom_qty_required for RM-1 = 2   (just qty_per_unit, no multiplication)
   897→✅ CORRECT:      bom_qty_required for RM-1 = 4   (2 units of A × 2 per unit = 4 total)
   898→                 bom_qty_required for RM-2 = 10  (2 units of A × 5 per unit = 10 total)
   899→```
   900→
   901→---
   902→
   903→## Final Simplified Design (Phase 4B Implementation)
   904→
   905→### Core Principles
   906→
   907→**Three fundamental rules that drive the entire system:**
   908→
   909→1. **`project_qty`** = **ALWAYS manually entered** (never auto-calculated)
   910→   - User enters it OR comes from Excel column E
   911→   - System NEVER overwrites this value
   912→
   913→2. **`bom_qty_required`** = **ALWAYS auto-calculated** from BOM usage
   914→   - Multiplies `qty_per_unit × parent.project_qty`
   915→   - Sums across all parent BOMs for multi-BOM usage
   916→
   917→3. **`total_qty_limit`** = **MAX(project_qty, bom_qty_required)**
   918→   - Simplified from original Decision 5 which used `loose_qty_required`
   919→   - Hard procurement limit enforced in Material Request validation
   920→

   964→
   965→### Implementation Details
   966→
   967→#### Fixed Calculation: `calculate_bom_qty_required()`
   968→
   969→**File:** `project_component_master.py`
   970→
   971→```python
   972→def calculate_bom_qty_required(self):
   973→    """
   974→    Calculate BOM quantity required by multiplying qty_per_unit by parent's project_qty.
   975→
   976→    For root assemblies (no bom_usage): bom_qty_required = project_qty
   977→    For loose items "Pending Conversion": bom_qty_required = 0
   978→    For components in BOMs: SUM(parent.project_qty × qty_per_unit)
   979→    """
   980→    if not self.bom_usage:
   981→        # Root assembly or loose item - no BOM usage rows
   982→        if self.is_loose_item:
   983→            self.bom_qty_required = 0  # Not in any BOM yet
   984→        else:
   985→            self.bom_qty_required = self.project_qty or 0  # Root assembly
   986→        return
   987→
   988→    # Has BOM usage rows - calculate from all parent BOMs
   989→    total = 0
   990→    for usage in self.bom_usage:
   991→        # Get parent's manually-set project quantity
   992→        parent_qty = frappe.db.get_value(
   993→            "Project Component Master",
   994→            {"project": self.project, "item_code": usage.parent_item},
   995→            "project_qty"
   996→        ) or 0
   997→
   998→        # Multiply and store row-level total
   999→        usage.total_qty_required = (usage.qty_per_unit or 0) * parent_qty
  1000→        total += usage.total_qty_required
  1001→
  1002→    self.bom_qty_required = total
  1003→```
  1004→
  1005→**Key Changes:**
  1006→- ✅ Multiplies `qty_per_unit × parent_project_qty` (fixes the bug!)
  1007→- ✅ Populates `total_qty_required` on each BOM Usage row
  1008→- ✅ Handles root assemblies, loose items, and child components correctly
  1009→
  1010→#### Simplified: `calculate_total_qty_limit()`
  1011→
  1012→**Original (Decision 5):**
  1013→```python
  1014→total_qty_limit = MAX(loose_qty_required, bom_qty_required)
  1015→```
  1016→
  1017→**New (Simplified):**
  1018→```python
  1019→def calculate_total_qty_limit(self):
  1020→    """MAX(project_qty, bom_qty_required) - hard procurement limit."""
  1021→    project = self.project_qty or 0
  1022→    bom = self.bom_qty_required or 0
  1023→    self.total_qty_limit = max(project, bom)
  1024→```
  1025→
  1026→**Rationale:** User's `project_qty` estimate should override if higher than BOM requirement
  1027→
  1028→**Removed:** `calculate_project_qty()` method entirely (project_qty is always manual!)
  1029→
  1030→---
  1031→
  1032→### Multi-Level Calculation Example
  1033→
  1034→```
  1035→Project Structure (from Excel upload):
  1036→
  1037→TOP-ASSY
  1038→├── project_qty = 3 (from Excel column E)
  1039→├── bom_qty_required = 3 (no bom_usage, so equals project_qty)
  1040→├── total_qty_limit = MAX(3, 3) = 3
  1041→
  1042→SUB-ASSY (child of TOP-ASSY)
  1043→├── qty_per_unit = 2 (in TOP-ASSY's BOM)
  1044→├── project_qty = 0 (user doesn't set for child items)
  1045→├── bom_usage table:
  1046→│   └── parent=TOP-ASSY, qty_per_unit=2, total_qty_required=6 (3×2)
  1047→├── bom_qty_required = 6 (auto-calculated)
  1048→├── total_qty_limit = MAX(0, 6) = 6
  1049→
  1050→MOTOR (child of SUB-ASSY, used in 2 BOMs)
  1051→├── qty_per_unit = 1 (in SUB-ASSY's BOM)
  1052→├── qty_per_unit = 2 (in PUMP-ASSY's BOM, assume PUMP project_qty=4)
  1053→├── project_qty = 0
  1054→├── bom_usage table (2 rows):
  1055→│   ├── parent=SUB-ASSY, qty_per_unit=1, total_qty_required=6 (6×1)
  1056→│   └── parent=PUMP-ASSY, qty_per_unit=2, total_qty_required=8 (4×2)
  1057→├── bom_qty_required = 6 + 8 = 14 (SUM approach for multi-BOM)
  1058→├── total_qty_limit = MAX(0, 14) = 14
  1059→```
  1060→
  1061→**Note:** For child items, users typically don't set `project_qty` (leave at 0), so `total_qty_limit` uses `bom_qty_required`.
  1062→
  1063→---
  1064→
  1065→### Loose Item Handling (All 4 Conversion Statuses)
  1066→
  1067→**Status 1: "Not Applicable"** (`is_loose_item = 0`)
  1068→- Regular assembly/component
  1069→- Follows standard calculation logic
  1070→
  1071→**Status 2: "Pending Conversion"** (`is_loose_item = 1`, no bom_usage)
  1072→```
  1073→Bearing-SKF:
  1074→├── is_loose_item = 1
  1075→├── project_qty = 150 (user's estimate, manually entered)
  1076→├── loose_qty_required = 100 (user decides to procure this much upfront)
  1077→├── bom_usage = empty (not in any BOM yet)
  1078→├── bom_qty_required = 0 (calculation sets to 0 for loose items)
  1079→├── total_qty_limit = MAX(150, 0) = 150
  1080→└── Result: Can procure up to 150 (user's estimate is the limit)
  1081→```
  1082→
  1083→**Status 3: "Converted to BOM"** (`is_loose_item = 1`, 1 bom_usage row)
  1084→```
  1085→Bearing-SKF now added to Motor-Assembly BOM:
  1086→├── is_loose_item = 1 (preserves history)
  1087→├── project_qty = 150 (unchanged, user's original estimate)
  1088→├── loose_qty_required = 100 (historical loose procurement)
  1089→├── bom_usage: parent=Motor-Assembly (project_qty=50), qty_per_unit=2
  1090→├── bom_qty_required = 2 × 50 = 100 (auto-calculated from BOM)
  1091→├── total_qty_limit = MAX(150, 100) = 150
  1092→└── Result: User's estimate (150) wins! Prevents duplicate procurement.
  1093→```
  1094→
  1095→**Status 4: "Partial"** (`is_loose_item = 1`, 2+ bom_usage rows)
  1096→```
  1097→Bearing-SKF used in 2 assemblies:
  1098→├── is_loose_item = 1
  1099→├── project_qty = 150
  1100→├── bom_usage (2 rows):
  1101→│   ├── Motor-Assembly (project_qty=50) × qty_per_unit=2 = 100
  1102→│   └── Pump-Assembly (project_qty=30) × qty_per_unit=1 = 30
  1103→├── bom_qty_required = 100 + 30 = 130 (SUM approach)
  1104→├── total_qty_limit = MAX(150, 130) = 150
  1105→└── Result: User's estimate still wins. Loose procurement covers both BOMs.
  1106→```
  1107→
  1108→**Key Insight:** Loose items use `project_qty` for procurement planning even before BOM exists, preventing over-procurement when later converted.
  1109→
  1110→---
  1111→
  1112→### Three Data Flows
  1113→
  1114→#### Flow 1: BOM Upload (New Projects)
  1115→
  1116→**Sequence:**
  1117→```
  1118→1. User uploads Excel with column E (quantity)
  1119→2. bom_upload_enhanced.py parses Excel
  1120→3. For each assembly:
  1121→   - Sets project_qty from column E ✅
  1122→   - Sets created_from = "BOM Upload" ✅
  1123→   - project_qty becomes READ-ONLY (data integrity) ✅
  1124→4. Creates BOMs bottom-up
  1125→5. BOM hooks populate bom_usage tables
  1126→6. Save triggers:
  1127→   - calculate_bom_qty_required() multiplies project_qty × qty_per_unit ✅
  1128→   - calculate_total_qty_limit() = MAX(project_qty, bom_qty_required) ✅
  1129→```
  1130→
  1131→**Data Integrity Protection:**
  1132→- `project_qty` read-only for BOM Upload source
  1133→- User cannot accidentally change Excel values
  1134→- Forces proper workflow: update PE2 → re-upload
  1135→- Leverages existing duplicate BOM blocking
  1136→
  1137→#### Flow 2: Cutover (Existing Projects)
  1138→
  1139→**Sequence:**
  1140→```
  1141→1. User clicks "Generate Component Masters" on Project
  1142→2. bulk_generation.py creates CMs from existing BOMs:
  1143→   - Sets project_qty = 1 (default) ✅
  1144→   - Sets created_from = "Cutover" ✅
  1145→   - project_qty stays EDITABLE (needs review) ✅
  1146→3. Warning shown: "Review and update Project Qty Required"
  1147→4. User manually updates root assembly project_qty to correct value
  1148→5. Save triggers recalculation cascade:
  1149→   - Root assembly: bom_qty_required = project_qty
  1150→   - Children: bom_qty_required = SUM(parent_qty × qty_per_unit)
  1151→   - total_qty_limit = MAX(project_qty, bom_qty_required)
  1152→```
  1153→
  1154→**Cutover Limitations:**
  1155→- Root assemblies have NO bom_usage rows (nothing references them)
  1156→- Raw materials get NO Component Masters (only assemblies with BOMs)
  1157→- Full multiplication chain requires user to set root project_qty
  1158→- System defaults to 1 (safe but incomplete)
  1159→
  1160→#### Flow 3: Manual Entry
  1161→
  1162→**Sequence:**
  1163→```
  1164→1. User creates Component Master directly in ERPNext
  1165→2. Sets created_from = "Manual Entry" manually
  1166→3. project_qty fully editable (no restrictions)
  1167→4. User enters all values including project_qty
  1168→5. Save triggers normal calculations
  1169→```
  1170→
  1171→**Use Case:** Loose items created before BOM exists
  1172→
  1173→---
  1174→

  1176→
  1177→1. **project_component_master.json**
  1178→   - Added `project_qty` field (Float, conditional read-only)
  1179→   - Added `created_from` field (Select, tracking)
  1180→   - Updated `total_qty_limit` description
  1181→
  1182→2. **project_component_master.py**
  1183→   - ✅ Fixed `calculate_bom_qty_required()` to multiply
  1184→   - ✅ Updated `calculate_total_qty_limit()` to MAX(project_qty, bom_qty)
  1185→   - ❌ Removed `calculate_project_qty()` method entirely
  1186→   - ❌ Removed call to calculate_project_qty from before_save()
  1187→
  1188→3. **bom_upload_enhanced.py**
  1189→   - Sets `project_qty` from Excel column E
  1190→   - Sets `created_from = "BOM Upload"`
  1191→
  1192→4. **bulk_generation.py**

  1194→   - Sets `created_from = "Cutover"`
  1195→   - Shows warning message to review quantities
  1196→
  1197→---
  1198→
  1199→### Design Rationale Summary
  1200→
  1201→**Why project_qty is always manual:**
  1202→- ✅ Clear separation: manual input vs calculated output
  1203→- ✅ No auto-overwrite confusion (user retains control)
  1204→- ✅ Simpler code (no complex auto-calculation logic)
  1205→- ✅ User can override procurement limits when needed
  1206→- ✅ Works for all flows: new uploads, cutover, loose items, manual entry
  1207→
  1208→**Why read-only for BOM Upload:**
  1209→- ✅ PE2 is source of truth, ERPNext reflects it
  1210→- ✅ Prevents accidental data corruption
  1211→- ✅ Forces proper workflow: fix PE2 → re-upload
  1212→- ✅ Leverages existing duplicate BOM blocking
  1213→- ✅ Clear signal: "this is managed by upload"
  1214→
  1215→**Why MAX(project_qty, bom_qty_required):**
  1216→- ✅ Simpler than original Decision 5
  1217→- ✅ User's estimate overrides if higher
  1218→- ✅ Handles loose items before/after BOM conversion
  1219→- ✅ Prevents over-procurement
  1220→- ✅ Single procurement limit for all sources
  1221→
  1222→---
  1223→
  1224→### Decision 14: Make/Buy Flag and Procurement Routing
  1225→
  1226→**Problem:** In ETO manufacturing, not all items in a BOM tree are procured the same way:
  1227→- Root machines (M level) and sub-assemblies (G level) are **assembled in-house**
  1228→- Some sub-assemblies (D level) are **procured as complete units** from vendors
  1229→- Raw materials (RM level) are **procured** unless their parent assembly is bought as a unit
  1230→
  1231→**When a parent assembly is "Buy" (procured as unit):**
  1232→- The parent itself appears in MR/PO
  1233→- Its child RMs do NOT need separate procurement (they come with the assembly)
  1234→
  1235→**When a parent assembly is "Make" (assembled in-house):**
  1236→- The parent does NOT appear in MR/PO
  1237→- Its child RMs DO need separate procurement via MR/PO
  1238→
  1239→---
  1240→
  1241→#### Make/Buy Field Design
  1242→
  1243→```json
  1244→{
  1245→  "fieldname": "make_or_buy",
  1246→  "fieldtype": "Select",
  1247→  "label": "Make / Buy",
  1248→  "options": "\nMake\nBuy",
  1249→  "description": "Make = assembled in-house (children procured separately). Buy = procured as unit."
  1250→}
  1251→```
  1252→
  1253→**Defaults by item type:**
  1254→| Level | Typical Default | Notes |
  1255→|-------|----------------|-------|
  1256→| M (Root Machine) | Make | Always assembled in-house |
  1257→| G (Sub-Assembly) | Make | In-house (vendors not yet developed) |
  1258→| D (Sub-Assembly) | Make or Buy | Depends on vendor availability per project |
  1259→| RM (Raw Material) | Buy | Always procured |
  1260→
  1261→**Editability:**
  1262→- ✅ Editable directly on Component Master form (anytime, including mid-project)
  1263→- ✅ Can change mid-project (e.g., vendor fails → switch D from Buy to Make)
  1264→- ✅ Set from BOM Upload Excel column (if PE2 team adds the column)
  1265→
  1266→---
  1267→
  1268→#### BOM Upload Merge Logic for Make/Buy
  1269→
  1270→PE2 exports the full BOM file every time. Previously tagged items should NOT require re-tagging.
  1271→
  1272→```python
  1273→# Merge strategy during BOM Upload:
  1274→if excel_has_make_buy_value:       # Excel column has value
  1275→    component.make_or_buy = excel_value    # Use Excel value
  1276→elif component_already_exists:     # Existing CM, Excel column empty
  1277→    pass                           # Keep existing value (no change)
  1278→elif new_component_with_bom:       # New item with children
  1279→    component.make_or_buy = "Make" # Default for assemblies
  1280→elif new_component_without_bom:    # New leaf item (RM)
  1281→    component.make_or_buy = "Buy"  # Default for raw materials
  1282→```
  1283→
  1284→**Key behavior:** PE2 team only tags new items or changes. Previously tagged items can be left blank in Excel.
  1285→
  1286→---
  1287→
  1288→#### Impact on Quantity Calculations
  1289→
  1290→**CRITICAL:** Parent's make/buy flag determines whether children need separate procurement.
  1291→
  1292→```
  1293→M (Make) — project_qty = 2
  1294→├─ G1 (Make)
  1295→│   ├─ D1 (Buy)  ← Procured as unit. D1's RMs covered!
  1296→│   │   ├─ RM-1  ← SKIP (parent D1 is Buy, covers RM-1)
  1297→│   │   └─ RM-2  ← SKIP (parent D1 is Buy, covers RM-2)
  1298→│   └─ D2 (Make) ← In-house. D2's RMs need separate procurement!
  1299→│       ├─ RM-1  ← COUNT (parent D2 is Make, RM-1 needs own MR)
  1300→│       └─ RM-3  ← COUNT (parent D2 is Make, RM-3 needs own MR)
  1301→```
  1302→
  1303→**Updated bom_qty_required calculation:**
  1304→```python
  1305→def calculate_bom_qty_required(self):
  1306→    total = 0
  1307→    for usage in self.bom_usage:
  1308→        parent = frappe.db.get_value(
  1309→            "Project Component Master",
  1310→            {"project": self.project, "item_code": usage.parent_item},
  1311→            ["project_qty", "make_or_buy"], as_dict=True
  1312→        )
  1313→
  1314→        # Only count if parent is "Make" (assembled in-house)
  1315→        # If parent is "Buy", this component is covered by parent's procurement
  1316→        if parent and parent.make_or_buy == "Make":
  1317→            usage.total_qty_required = (usage.qty_per_unit or 0) * (parent.project_qty or 0)
  1318→            total += usage.total_qty_required
  1319→        else:
  1320→            usage.total_qty_required = 0  # Covered by parent procurement
  1321→
  1322→    self.bom_qty_required = total
  1323→```
  1324→
  1325→---
  1326→
  1327→#### Cascade Recalculation on Make/Buy Change
  1328→
  1329→When make/buy changes mid-project, ALL children's `bom_qty_required` must recalculate:
  1330→
  1331→```
  1332→BEFORE: D1 = Buy
  1333→  RM-1.bom_qty_required → D1 excluded → only counts Make parents → 0
  1334→  RM-2.bom_qty_required → D1 excluded → 0
  1335→
  1336→AFTER: D1 changed to Make (vendor failed)
  1337→  RM-1.bom_qty_required → D1 now included → D1.project_qty × qty_per_unit
  1338→  RM-2.bom_qty_required → D1 now included → D1.project_qty × qty_per_unit
  1339→
  1340→  → Cascade: All children of D1 recalculate bom_qty_required!
  1341→```
  1342→
  1343→**Implementation:**
  1344→```python
  1345→def before_save(self):
  1346→    if self.has_value_changed("make_or_buy"):
  1347→        self.recalculate_children_bom_qty()
  1348→    # existing calculations...
  1349→
  1350→def recalculate_children_bom_qty(self):
  1351→    """When make/buy changes, trigger recalc on all BOM children."""
  1352→    if not self.active_bom:
  1353→        return
  1354→    bom_items = frappe.get_all("BOM Item",
  1355→        filters={"parent": self.active_bom},
  1356→        fields=["item_code"])
  1357→    for item in bom_items:
  1358→        child_cm = frappe.db.get_value(
  1359→            "Project Component Master",
  1360→            {"project": self.project, "item_code": item.item_code}, "name")
  1361→        if child_cm:
  1362→            child_doc = frappe.get_doc("Project Component Master", child_cm)
  1363→            child_doc.save()  # Triggers before_save → recalculates
  1364→```
  1365→
  1366→---
  1367→
  1368→#### Impact on Validation
  1369→
  1370→MR/PO validation only enforces limits for "Buy" items (items that are actually procured):
  1371→
  1372→```python
  1373→# In validate_material_request_qty / validate_purchase_order_qty:
  1374→component = get_component_master(project, item_code)
  1375→
  1376→if not component:
  1377→    return  # Not tracked
  1378→
  1379→if component.make_or_buy != "Buy":
  1380→    return  # "Make" items are not directly procured, skip validation
  1381→
  1382→# ... existing cumulative qty validation for Buy items
  1383→```
  1384→
  1385→---
  1386→
  1387→#### Impact on Reports
  1388→
  1389→**Report at M & G level with drill-down:**
  1390→```
  1391→Project: PROJ-2024-MACHINE-A
  1392→═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  1393→M-001 (Make)                           Overall: 65%
  1394→├─ G1 (Make)                           Procurement: 70%
  1395→│   ├─ D1 (Buy)     PO: 10/10 ✓       ← Direct procurement
  1396→│   │   (RMs covered by D1 purchase)
  1397→│   └─ D2 (Make)                       ← In-house
  1398→│       ├─ RM-3     PO: 150/200 (75%) ← Separate procurement
  1399→│       └─ RM-4     PO: 0/300 (0%)    ← Pending!
  1400→├─ G2 (Make)                           Procurement: 60%
  1401→│   └─ D3 (Buy)     PO: 8/15 (53%)    ← Direct procurement
  1402→│       (RMs covered by D3 purchase)
  1403→```
  1404→
  1405→**Report logic per node:**
  1406→- **Buy item:** Show its own procurement status
  1407→- **Make item:** Show rollup of children's procurement %
  1408→- **Buy item's children:** Skip (covered by parent's procurement)
  1409→
  1410→---
  1411→
  1412→#### Project Field Names by DocType
  1413→
  1414→| DocType | Project Field Name | Notes |
  1415→|---------|-------------------|-------|
  1416→| Material Request | `custom_project_` | Custom field (with trailing underscore) |
  1417→| Request for Quotation | `custom_project` | Custom field (no trailing underscore) |
  1418→| Purchase Order | `project` | Standard ERPNext field |
  1419→| Purchase Receipt | `project` | Standard ERPNext field |
  1420→
  1421→**Important:** Validation code must use the correct field name for each doctype.
  1422→
  1423→---
  1424→
  1425→### Decision 15: Why Keep Component Masters for Root/Sub-Assemblies (M & G Levels)
  1426→
  1427→**Question raised:** Since M and G levels are always "Make" (assembled in-house), what benefit do their Component Masters provide?
  1428→
  1429→**Answer: They are essential for 3 reasons:**
  1430→
  1431→**1. project_qty Storage and Propagation Chain**
  1432→```
  1433→M.project_qty = 2        ← WHERE the quantity originates (stored in Component Master)
  1434→│
  1435→├─ G.bom_qty_required = M.project_qty × qty_per_unit = 2 × 3 = 6
  1436→│  │
  1437→│  ├─ D.bom_qty_required = G.project_qty × qty_per_unit
  1438→│  │   │
  1439→│  │   └─ RM.bom_qty_required = D.project_qty × qty_per_unit
  1440→```
  1441→Without M and G Component Masters, the calculation chain breaks — there's no record to store root project_qty.
  1442→
  1443→**2. Report Entry Points**
  1444→Management wants: "Show me Machine M — how is procurement?" Component Master for M is the starting node for the tree drill-down report.
  1445→
  1446→**3. BOM Change Detection**
  1447→Already implemented — `bom_structure_hash` comparison during BOM Upload requires Component Master to exist for assemblies.
  1448→
  1449→**What they DON'T use:**
  1450→- Procurement validation (not procured, they're "Make")
  1451→- total_qty_procured (always 0)
  1452→- These fields sit empty — acceptable trade-off for consistent data model.
  1453→
  1454→---
  1455→
  1456→### Decision 16: BOM Version Change Handling (During BOM Upload)
  1457→
  1458→**Problem:** When a new BOM version is uploaded via PE2 Excel for an item that already has an active BOM in Component Master:
  1459→1. The old BOM's `bom_usage` entries remain on child Component Masters, double-counting qty
  1460→2. Items removed in the new BOM version still show procurement demand
  1461→3. Need to handle different procurement stages differently (no procurement vs MR vs RFQ vs PO)
  1462→
  1463→**Tiered Blocking Rules:**
  1464→
  1465→| Procurement Stage | Behavior | User Action |
  1466→|-------------------|----------|-------------|
  1467→| **No MR/RFQ/PO** | Confirm dialog with remarks | User confirms, keys in reason |
  1468→| **MR exists** | BLOCK | User deactivates old BOM manually |
  1469→| **RFQ exists** | BLOCK | User deactivates old BOM manually |
  1470→| **PO exists** | BLOCK (stricter) | Requires Manager role to proceed |
  1471→
  1472→---
  1473→
  1474→**Scenario A — No Procurement (Hash Changed, No MR/RFQ/PO):**
  1475→```
  1476→Item A has BOM-A-001 (active), no MRs/RFQs/POs for any children
  1477→User uploads PE2 file with changed BOM structure
  1478→
  1479→System behavior:
  1480→1. Detects hash change
  1481→2. Checks procurement status → none found
  1482→3. Shows confirm dialog:
  1483→   "BOM structure changed for Item A. Proceed with new version?"
  1484→   [Remarks: ________________]
  1485→   [Cancel] [Proceed]
  1486→4. If user confirms:
  1487→   - Stores remarks in Component Master (version_change_remarks field)
  1488→   - Old BOM's usage rows removed from children
  1489→   - New BOM created and linked
  1490→   - Proceeds with upload
  1491→5. If user cancels:
  1492→   - Upload blocked for this component and its ancestors
  1493→```
  1494→
  1495→**Scenario B — MR or RFQ Exists:**
  1496→```
  1497→Item A has BOM-A-001 (active), child X has MR-00123 submitted
  1498→User uploads PE2 file with changed BOM structure
  1499→
  1500→System behavior:
  1501→1. Detects hash change
  1502→2. Checks procurement status → MR found
  1503→3. Shows BLOCK dialog:
  1504→   "Cannot change BOM for Item A. Child items have active procurement:
  1505→    - X: MR-00123 (Submitted, qty: 50)
  1506→
  1507→   To proceed:
  1508→   1. Go to BOM List
  1509→   2. Deactivate BOM-A-001
  1510→   3. Re-run BOM Upload"
  1511→   [Open BOM List]
  1512→4. Upload blocked for this component and its ancestors
  1513→5. User manually deactivates old BOM
  1514→6. User re-runs upload → proceeds (no procurement on old BOM now)
  1515→```
  1516→
  1517→**Scenario C — PO Exists (Stricter):**
  1518→```
  1519→Item A has BOM-A-001 (active), child X has PO-00456 submitted
  1520→User uploads PE2 file with changed BOM structure
  1521→
  1522→System behavior:
  1523→1. Detects hash change
  1524→2. Checks procurement status → PO found
  1525→3. Checks user role:
  1526→   - If NOT "Component Master Manager" or "System Manager":
  1527→     Shows BLOCK dialog (same as Scenario B)
  1528→   - If HAS manager role:
  1529→     Shows confirm dialog with strong warning:
  1530→     "⚠️ CAUTION: Child items have active Purchase Orders!
  1531→      - X: PO-00456 (Submitted, qty: 50)
  1532→
  1533→      Proceeding will change the BOM. Existing POs will remain unchanged.
  1534→      [Remarks: ________________]
  1535→      [Cancel] [Proceed Anyway]"
  1536→```
  1537→
  1538→**Scenario D — BOM Cancelled:**
  1539→```
  1540→Item A has BOM-A-001 as active_bom
  1541→User cancels BOM-A-001
  1542→
  1543→System behavior:
  1544→1. System finds fallback BOM (active+default first, then any active)
  1545→2. If fallback found:
  1546→   - Sets active_bom = BOM-A-002
  1547→   - Populates bom_usage entries from fallback
  1548→   - Notifies user: "BOM-A-001 cancelled. Switched to BOM-A-002"
  1549→3. If no fallback:
  1550→   - Clears active_bom
  1551→   - has_bom remains 1 (item type unchanged)
  1552→```
  1553→
  1554→---
  1555→
  1556→**New Field for Remarks:**
  1557→```json
  1558→{
  1559→  "fieldname": "version_change_remarks",
  1560→  "fieldtype": "Small Text",
  1561→  "label": "Version Change Remarks",
  1562→  "description": "Reason for allowing BOM version change (logged during upload)",
  1563→  "read_only": 1
  1564→}
  1565→```
  1566→
  1567→**Implementation:**
  1568→```python
  1569→# In bom_upload_enhanced.py:
  1570→
  1571→def _check_procurement_blocking(project, item_code, old_bom_name):
  1572→    """
  1573→    Check if procurement exists and determine blocking level.
  1574→
  1575→    Returns:
  1576→        dict: {
  1577→            "can_proceed": bool,
  1578→            "blocking_level": "none" | "confirm" | "block" | "manager_required",
  1579→            "procurement_docs": [...],
  1580→            "message": str
  1581→        }
  1582→    """
  1583→    # Get all child items from old BOM
  1584→    old_bom = frappe.get_doc("BOM", old_bom_name)
  1585→
  1586→    blocking_level = "none"
  1587→    procurement_docs = []
  1588→
  1589→    for item in old_bom.items:
  1590→        # Check MR
  1591→        mrs = get_material_requests(project, item.item_code)
  1592→        if mrs:
  1593→            procurement_docs.extend(mrs)
  1594→            blocking_level = max_level(blocking_level, "block")
  1595→
  1596→        # Check RFQ
  1597→        rfqs = get_rfqs(project, item.item_code)
  1598→        if rfqs:
  1599→            procurement_docs.extend(rfqs)
  1600→            blocking_level = max_level(blocking_level, "block")
  1601→
  1602→        # Check PO
  1603→        pos = get_purchase_orders(project, item.item_code)
  1604→        if pos:
  1605→            procurement_docs.extend(pos)
  1606→            blocking_level = "manager_required"  # Strictest
  1607→
  1608→    return {
  1609→        "can_proceed": blocking_level == "none",
  1610→        "blocking_level": blocking_level,
  1611→        "procurement_docs": procurement_docs,
  1612→    }
  1613→
  1614→
  1615→def _can_override_po_block():
  1616→    """Check if current user has manager role for PO-stage override."""
  1617→    user_roles = frappe.get_roles()
  1618→    return "Component Master Manager" in user_roles or "System Manager" in user_roles
  1619→```
  1620→
  1621→**User-Facing Dialogs:**
  1622→
  1623→1. **Confirm Dialog (No Procurement):**
  1624→   - Title: "BOM Structure Changed"
  1625→   - Message: "BOM structure changed for {item}. Proceed with new version?"
  1626→   - Fields: Remarks (mandatory)
  1627→   - Buttons: [Cancel] [Proceed]
  1628→
  1629→2. **Block Dialog (MR/RFQ Exists):**
  1630→   - Title: "BOM Change Blocked"
  1631→   - Message: Lists procurement documents
  1632→   - Instructions: Deactivate old BOM, re-run upload
  1633→   - Button: [Open BOM List]
  1634→
  1635→3. **Manager Confirm Dialog (PO Exists):**
  1636→   - Title: "⚠️ BOM Change - Manager Override"
  1637→   - Message: Strong warning about existing POs
  1638→   - Fields: Remarks (mandatory)
  1639→   - Buttons: [Cancel] [Proceed Anyway]
  1640→   - Only shown to users with Manager role
  1641→
  1642→---
  1643→
  1644→## Design Evolution
  1645→
  1646→This section documents key design iterations during the architecture discussion.
  1647→
  1648→### Evolution 1: Component Master Scope
  1649→
  1650→**Initial Proposal:**
  1651→> "Create Component Master only for items having BOM (excluding loose items)"
  1652→
  1653→**Issue Identified:**
  1654→> "But loose items have no BOM, where do they live?"
  1655→
  1656→**Resolution:**
  1657→Changed scope to: `has_bom = Yes OR is_loose_item = Yes`
  1658→
  1659→**Learning:** Loose items are a special case requiring tracking despite being leaf nodes.
  1660→
  1661→---
  1662→
  1663→### Evolution 2: Procurement Quantity Logic
  1664→
  1665→**Initial Proposal:**
  1666→> "If loose=100 and BOM=50, error if MR qty > 100"
  1667→
  1668→**Issue Identified:**
  1669→> "What if loose=100 and BOM=150? Using MAX(100, 150)=150 is correct, but what about the 100 already procured?"
  1670→
  1671→**Resolution:**
  1672→- total_qty_limit = MAX(loose, bom)
  1673→- total_procurement = SUM(all MRs including loose)
  1674→- Block if: total_procurement > total_qty_limit
  1675→- Warning if: loose > bom (over-procurement alert)
  1676→
  1677→**Learning:** Need to track cumulative procurement across loose + BOM sources.
  1678→
  1679→---
  1680→
  1681→### Evolution 3: Change Blocking Granularity
  1682→
  1683→**Initial Proposal:**
  1684→> "Block entire upload until changes resolved"
  1685→
  1686→**Issue Identified:**
  1687→> "If 100 components uploaded and 2 changed, blocks 98 good components unnecessarily"
  1688→
  1689→**Resolution:**
  1690→Staged approach:
  1691→1. Categorize: new / unchanged / changed / blocked
  1692→2. Create new and unchanged immediately
  1693→3. Block only components dependent on changed items
  1694→4. Show resolution dialog for changed items
  1695→
  1696→**Learning:** Surgical blocking maintains efficiency while ensuring safety.
  1697→
  1698→---
  1699→
  1700→### Evolution 4: Loose Item State Model
  1701→
  1702→**Initial Proposal:**
  1703→> "is_loose_item becomes No after conversion"
  1704→
  1705→**Issue Identified:**
  1706→> "Loses procurement history and can't explain why total_limit > bom_qty"
  1707→
  1708→**Resolution:**
  1709→- Keep `is_loose_item = Yes` permanently
  1710→- Add `bom_conversion_status` field (Pending / Converted / Partial)
  1711→- Loose vs BOM procurement tracked separately in child table
  1712→
  1713→**Learning:** State transitions, not binary flags, better model complex workflows.
  1714→
  1715→---
  1716→
  1717→### Evolution 5: Budget Tracking Complexity
  1718→
  1719→**Initial Proposal:**
  1720→> "Budget at component level and leaf level in child table"
  1721→
  1722→**Issue Identified:**
  1723→> "Child table duplicates BOM structure, sync nightmare, high maintenance"
  1724→
  1725→**Resolution:**
  1726→- Budget only for managed components (assemblies + loose)
  1727→- Rollup uses: managed component budgets + leaf item actual costs
  1728→- Matches business practice (designers estimate at assembly level)
  1729→
  1730→**Learning:** Simplicity over completeness when complexity doesn't add business value.
  1731→
  1732→---
  1733→
  1734→### Evolution 6: Component Master Scope Expanded to ALL Items
  1735→
  1736→**Initial Design (v1.0-v1.7):**
  1737→> "Component Masters only for assemblies (has_bom=1) and loose items (is_loose_item=1). Raw materials are just BOM Items."
  1738→
  1739→**Issue Identified:**
  1740→> "Raw materials are not present in Component Masters. MR/PO validation can't enforce limits on RM procurement. Report rollup from M/G level needs ALL levels to exist."
  1741→
  1742→**Resolution:**
  1743→- Component Masters created for ALL items in BOM tree (assemblies + raw materials)
  1744→- Raw materials: has_bom=0, make_or_buy=Buy
  1745→- Enables: procurement validation, report rollup, calculation chain
  1746→- BOM Upload modified to create Component Masters for leaf items too
  1747→
  1748→**Learning:** Procurement control requires visibility at every level. Tracking only assemblies left a gap at the most procured level (raw materials).
  1749→
  1750→---
  1751→
  1752→### Evolution 7: Make/Buy Flag for Procurement Routing
  1753→
  1754→**Initial Design:**
  1755→> "All items in Component Master follow same procurement rules."
  1756→
  1757→**Issue Identified:**
  1758→> "If D-level sub-assembly is procured as whole unit (Buy), its raw materials should NOT be separately procured. If D is assembled in-house (Make), its RMs need separate MRs."
  1759→
  1760→**Resolution:**
  1761→- Added `make_or_buy` field (Select: Make/Buy)
  1762→- Editable anytime on Component Master form (mid-project changes supported)
  1763→- BOM Upload merge: Excel value wins if present, else keep existing
  1764→- bom_qty_required calculation only counts "Make" parents
  1765→- MR/PO validation only enforces for "Buy" items
  1766→- Cascade recalculation when make/buy changes on parent
  1767→
  1768→**Learning:** Procurement routing depends on manufacturing strategy, not just BOM structure. The same BOM tree can have different procurement patterns depending on vendor availability.
  1769→
  1770→---
  1771→
  1772→## Final Architecture
  1773→
  1774→### System Components
  1775→
  1776→```
  1777→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  1778→│                     BOM Upload (Excel)                       │
  1779→│                  (PE2 Export, ~500 rows)                     │
  1780→└───────────────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────┘
  1781→                            │
  1782→                            ▼
  1783→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  1784→│              BOM Upload Analysis Engine                      │
  1785→│  Step 1: Parse Excel → Build tree                            │
  1786→│  Step 2: Create Component Masters FIRST (active_bom=null)    │
  1787→│  Step 3: Analyze — compare hashes, check procurement blocks  │
  1788→│  Step 4: Categorize: new / unchanged / changed / blocked     │
  1789→└───────────┬─────────────────────────────────┬───────────────┘
  1790→            │                                 │
  1791→            │ If blocked/changed              │ If clear
  1792→            ▼                                 ▼
  1793→┌─────────────────────────┐   ┌──────────────────────────────┐
  1794→│ Change Resolution Dialog│   │  Create BOMs (bottom-up)     │
  1795→│  • Show changes          │   │  • Use existing BOM Upload    │
  1796→│  • List blocked items    │   │    logic for BOM creation    │
  1797→│  • Require user action   │   │  • Link active_bom back to   │
  1798→│                          │   │    Component Masters         │
  1799→└─────────────────────────┘   │  • BOM hooks auto-populate    │
  1800→                               │    BOM Usage tables           │
  1801→                               └────────────┬─────────────────┘
  1802→                                            │
  1803→                                            ▼
  1804→                    ┌───────────────────────────────────────┐
  1805→                    │   Project Component Master            │
  1806→                    │   • Tracks managed components         │
  1807→                    │   • Budget & timeline                 │
  1808→                    │   • Procurement status                │
  1809→                    │   • Quantity limits                   │
  1810→                    └───────────┬───────────────────────────┘
  1811→                                │
  1812→                                ▼
  1813→                    ┌───────────────────────────────────────┐
  1814→                    │   Material Request Validation         │
  1815→                    │   • Check quantity limits             │
  1816→                    │   • Enforce MAX(loose, bom)           │
  1817→                    │   • Hard block if exceeded            │
  1818→                    └───────────┬───────────────────────────┘
  1819→                                │
  1820→                                ▼
  1821→                    ┌───────────────────────────────────────┐
  1822→                    │   Standard ERPNext Procurement        │
  1823→                    │   MR → RFQ → Quote → PO → Receipt     │
  1824→                    └───────────────────────────────────────┘
  1825→```
  1826→
  1827→### Data Flow
  1828→
  1829→**Scenario: Initial Upload (Day 1) — New Project via PE2**
  1830→```
  1831→1. Upload BOM Excel → 2 assemblies, 10 leaf items
  1832→2. Create Items for assemblies (ensure_item_exists — idempotent):
  1833→   - 2 Item masters for assembly nodes (Link field dependency)
  1834→3. Create Component Masters:
  1835→   - 2 Component Master entries (assemblies only)
  1836→   - has_bom=1, active_bom=null, design_status="Design Released"
  1837→4. Analysis: All new, no blocking issues
  1838→5. Create BOMs bottom-up (create_bom_recursive):
  1839→   - 2 BOM structures (with 10 leaf items in BOM Items)
  1840→   - 10 Item masters for leaf items (ensure_item_exists called again, idempotent)
  1841→6. Link back: Set active_bom and bom_structure_hash on Component Masters
  1842→7. BOM on_submit hooks populate BOM Usage tables
  1843→8. Final state: Design Released, Procurement Not Started
  1844→```
  1845→
  1846→**Scenario: Loose Item Procurement (Day 5)**
  1847→```
  1848→1. User manually creates Component Master entry
  1849→   - item_code: Bearing-SKF-001
  1850→   - is_loose_item: Yes
  1851→   - loose_qty_required: 100
  1852→   - loose_item_reason: "Long lead time, design pending"
  1853→2. Create Material Request: 100 units
  1854→3. Component Master auto-updates:
  1855→   - total_qty_limit: 100
  1856→   - total_qty_procured: 100
  1857→   - procurement_status: In Progress
  1858→```
  1859→
  1860→**Scenario: Incremental Upload (Day 30)**
  1861→```
  1862→1. Upload BOM Excel → 5 assemblies total
  1863→2. Analysis:
  1864→   - 2 unchanged (skip)
  1865→   - 2 new (create)
  1866→   - 1 changed (Gearbox structure modified)
  1867→3. Check dependencies:
  1868→   - Motor-Assembly uses Gearbox → Block Motor
  1869→   - Pump-Assembly doesn't use Gearbox → Allow
  1870→4. Show dialog:
  1871→   - "Gearbox changed, blocks Motor-Assembly"
  1872→   - "Create 2 new components (Pump-Assembly, Valve-Block)?"
  1873→5. User resolves Gearbox change (deactivates old BOM)
  1874→6. Re-run upload → Creates all remaining components
  1875→```
  1876→
  1877→**Scenario: Material Request with Limit Check (Day 35)**
  1878→```
  1879→1. User creates MR from Motor-Assembly BOM
  1880→2. Includes Bearing-SKF-001 (qty 50)
  1881→3. Validation hook:
  1882→   - Checks Component Master
  1883→   - loose_qty_required: 100
  1884→   - bom_qty_required: 50 (from Motor BOM)
  1885→   - total_qty_limit: MAX(100, 50) = 100
  1886→   - Existing procurement: 100 (loose MR)
  1887→   - This MR would add: 50
  1888→   - Total: 150 > 100 ✗
  1889→4. Error: "Cannot exceed limit of 100. Already procured 100 as loose item."
  1890→5. User adjusts: Remove Bearing from MR (already covered by loose procurement)
  1891→```
  1892→
  1893→---
  1894→
  1895→## Success Criteria
  1896→
  1897→The architecture is successful if it achieves:
  1898→
  1899→### Functional Goals
  1900→1. ✓ Incremental BOM uploads without manual tracking
  1901→2. ✓ Change detection with impact analysis
  1902→3. ✓ Loose item integration without duplicate procurement
  1903→4. ✓ Procurement quantity control enforcement
  1904→5. ✓ Budget and timeline visibility per component
  1905→6. ✓ Audit trail of BOM versions and changes
  1906→
  1907→### Non-Functional Goals
  1908→1. ✓ Maintainable: Clear separation of concerns (upload logic, validation, reporting)
  1909→2. ✓ Extensible: Can add new procurement controls without core changes
  1910→3. ✓ Performant: Analysis on 500-component BOM < 30 seconds
  1911→4. ✓ User-friendly: Clear error messages, actionable dialogs
  1912→5. ✓ Auditable: Full history of changes, decisions, procurement
  1913→
  1914→### Business Outcomes
  1915→1. Reduced over-procurement (cost savings)
  1916→2. Faster project execution (parallel work on independent components)
  1917→3. Better cost control (budget tracking and variance analysis)
  1918→4. Improved visibility (management dashboards and reports)
  1919→5. Reduced manual coordination (system enforces rules)
  1920→
  1921→---

  1947→
  1948→## Appendix: Key Design Principles
  1949→
  1950→### 1. Explicit Over Implicit
  1951→- Loose items require explicit conversion enabling
  1952→- Changes require explicit user resolution
  1953→- No silent auto-corrections
  1954→
  1955→### 2. Fail Fast and Loud
  1956→- Hard blocks on validation failures
  1957→- Clear error messages with actionable steps
  1958→- Better to stop early than corrupt data
  1959→
  1960→### 3. Single Source of Truth
  1961→- Component Master is THE record for project-specific component data
  1962→- Other doctypes (Item, BOM) remain global/reusable
  1963→- Clear ownership boundaries
  1964→
  1965→### 4. Business Logic in Code, Not User Memory
  1966→- System enforces quantity limits (not user discipline)
  1967→- System detects changes (not user tracking)
  1968→- System calculates rollups (not spreadsheets)
  1969→
  1970→### 5. Optimize for Common Case
  1971→- 90% of uploads are incremental (mostly new, few changed)
  1972→- Allow fast path for common case
  1973→- Slow path only for exceptions
  1974→
  1975→---
  1976→
  1977→## Conclusion
  1978→
  1979→The **Project Component Master** architecture provides a robust, maintainable solution for Clevertech's complex ETO procurement workflow. By introducing a project-specific component tracking layer, the system bridges the gap between design evolution (PE2) and procurement execution (ERPNext).
  1980→
  1981→Key innovations:
  1982→- **Full BOM tree tracking** (assemblies + raw materials, all levels)
  1983→- **Make/Buy routing** (parent's flag determines children's procurement path)
  1984→- **MAX-based quantity limits** (preventing duplicate procurement)
  1985→- **Ancestor chain blocking** (surgical change control)
  1986→- **Hash-based change detection** (fast and reliable)
  1987→- **Three-layer procurement validation** (MR + RFQ + PO with cumulative checks)
  1988→- **BOM version change handling** (old version cleanup, fallback on cancel, procurement impact warnings)
  1989→
  1990→The design balances flexibility (supports continuous design evolution) with control (enforces procurement limits), enabling Clevertech to scale their ETO operations efficiently.
  1991→
